basketball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.explanatio: A successful shot from beyond the three-point arc is worth 3 points.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.name: Basketball Rules & Positions
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.description: Dive deeper into fouls, violations, and player positions.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.title: Fouls & Violations
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.conte: A personal foul is illegal physical contact (pushing, holding, hitting). After 5 personal fouls a player is fouled out. Travelling means moving your feet without dribbling. Double dribble is when you stop dribbling and start again. A shot clock violation occurs when a team fails to attempt a shot within the allowed time (24 seconds in FIBA rules).
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.title: Player Positions
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.conte: The 5 positions are: Point Guard (PG) — the team's main ball handler and playmaker. Shooting Guard (SG) — typically the best outside shooter. Small Forward (SF) — a versatile scorer. Power Forward (PF) — plays near the basket, strong rebounder. Center (C) — the tallest player, dominates the paint area.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.question: How many personal fouls can a player commit before being fouled out (FIBA)?
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.explanat: In FIBA rules, a player is fouled out after 5 personal fouls.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.question: What is 'travelling' in basketball?
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.option1: Moving your feet without dribbling the ball
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.explanat: Travelling is when a player moves one or both feet illegally without dribbling.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.question: What is the shot clock duration in FIBA rules?
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.explanat: In FIBA basketball, teams have 24 seconds to attempt a shot.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.question: Which position is typically the team's main ball handler?
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.explanat: The Point Guard (PG) is the primary ball handler and playmaker.
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.question: What is a 'double dribble'?
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.option1: Stopping your dribble and then starting to dribble again
basketball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.explanat: A double dribble occurs when a player stops dribbling and then dribbles again.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.name: Basketball Advanced Knowledge
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.description: Test your knowledge on advanced rules, history, and tactics.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.title: History & Records
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.content: Basketball was invented in 1891 by Dr. James Naismith in Springfield, Massachusetts, USA. He used a peach basket and a soccer ball. The NBA was founded in 1946. Basketball became an official Olympic sport in 1936 at the Berlin Games. The most points ever scored in a single NBA game is 100, by Wilt Chamberlain in 1962.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.title: Advanced Rules & Tactics
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.content: A technical foul is given for unsportsmanlike conduct (arguing with refs, taunting). Zone defense means each player guards an area, not a specific opponent. In a man-to-man defense each player is assigned one opponent. The 'pick and roll' is an offensive play where one player sets a screen (pick) and then moves toward the basket (roll).
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.question: In which year was basketball invented?
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.explanatio: Dr. James Naismith invented basketball in 1891.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.question: What was used as the first basketball hoop?
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.explanatio: The first 'hoops' were actually peach baskets nailed to the wall.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.question: What is a 'technical foul'?
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option1: A foul for unsportsmanlike conduct like arguing with referees
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option2: A foul for pushing an opponent
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option3: A foul for stepping out of bounds
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.explanatio: A technical foul is called for unsportsmanlike behavior, not physical contact.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.question: In which year did basketball become an Olympic sport?
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.explanatio: Basketball debuted as an official Olympic sport at the 1936 Berlin Games.
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.question: What is a 'pick and roll' play?
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option2: One player sets a screen, then moves toward the basket
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option3: A fast break with all five players
basketball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.explanatio: In a pick and roll, one player sets a screen (pick) for the ball handler, then cuts toward the basket (roll).
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.name: Soccer Basics
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.description: Test your knowledge of basic soccer rules and facts.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.info1.title: The Game
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.info1.content: Soccer (football) is played between two teams of 11 players on a rectangular field with a goal at each end. A standard match lasts 90 minutes, split into two 45-minute halves. The objective is to score by getting the ball into the opposing team's goal. Only the goalkeeper may use their hands inside the penalty area.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.info2.title: Basic Rules
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.info2.content: The game is started and restarted with a kick-off from the center circle. If the ball crosses the sideline, the opposing team takes a throw-in. A corner kick is awarded when a defender last touches the ball before it crosses the goal line. A goal kick is awarded when an attacker last touches the ball before it crosses the goal line.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q1.question: How many players per team are on the field during a soccer match?
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q1.explanation: A soccer team has 11 players on the field, including the goalkeeper.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q2.question: How long is a standard soccer match?
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q2.explanation: A standard match is 90 minutes long, divided into two 45-minute halves.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q3.question: Which player is allowed to use their hands during play?
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q3.option1: The goalkeeper inside the penalty area
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q3.explanation: Only the goalkeeper may handle the ball, and only within their own penalty area.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q4.question: What is awarded when the ball goes over the sideline?
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q4.explanation: When the ball crosses the sideline (touchline), the opposing team takes a throw-in.
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q5.question: What shape is a soccer field?
soccer.quiz.rules-easy.q5.explanation: A soccer field is rectangular, typically 100-110 meters long and 64-75 meters wide.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.name: Soccer Rules & Positions
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.description: Dive deeper into soccer rules, positions, and set pieces.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.info1.title: Positions & Formation
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.info1.content: Soccer positions include goalkeeper, defenders, midfielders, and forwards. Common formations are described by numbers (e.g., 4-3-3 means 4 defenders, 3 midfielders, 3 forwards). The sweeper (libero) plays behind the defensive line. A striker's main job is to score goals, while a defensive midfielder protects the back line.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.info2.title: Fouls & Set Pieces
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.info2.content: A direct free kick is awarded for contact fouls (tripping, pushing, holding). A penalty kick is given when a foul occurs inside the penalty area. The penalty spot is 11 meters from the goal. A yellow card is a caution; two yellows equal a red card, which means expulsion. An indirect free kick is awarded for non-contact offenses like obstruction.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q1.question: How far is the penalty spot from the goal line?
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q1.explanation: The penalty spot is exactly 11 meters (about 12 yards) from the goal line.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q2.question: What does a yellow card mean?
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q2.option4: The player must sit out for 2 minutes
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q2.explanation: A yellow card is a caution. Receiving two yellow cards in one match results in a red card and expulsion.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q3.question: In a 4-3-3 formation, how many defenders are there?
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q3.explanation: In a 4-3-3 formation, the first number (4) represents the defenders.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.question: When is a penalty kick awarded?
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.option1: When a player is fouled anywhere on the field
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.option2: When the ball goes out of bounds
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.option3: When a player receives a yellow card
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.option4: When a foul is committed inside the penalty area
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q4.explanation: A penalty kick is awarded when a direct free kick foul occurs inside the offending team's penalty area.
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.question: What is the offside rule?
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.option1: A player cannot be closer to the opponent's goal than both the ball and the second-to-last defender when the ball is played to them
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.option2: A player cannot stand behind the goal
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.option3: A player cannot be in the penalty area for more than 5 seconds
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.option4: A player must stay on their own half
soccer.quiz.rules-medium.q5.explanation: A player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal than both the ball and the second-to-last defender at the moment the ball is played to them.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.name: Soccer Advanced Knowledge
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.description: Test your knowledge of soccer history, advanced rules, and tactics.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.info1.title: History & World Cup
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.info1.content: Modern soccer rules were established in 1863 when the Football Association (FA) was founded in England. The first FIFA World Cup was held in 1930 in Uruguay. Brazil holds the record for most World Cup titles with 5 wins. The FIFA Laws of the Game are maintained by the International Football Association Board (IFAB).
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.info2.title: Advanced Tactics
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.info2.content: Tiki-taka is a style of play involving short passing and movement, popularized by FC Barcelona and Spain. The 'false 9' is a tactical role where the center forward drops deep into midfield. Counter-attacking football focuses on quick transitions from defense to attack. Pressing means collectively pressuring the opponent high up the field to win back possession quickly.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q1.question: In which year was the first FIFA World Cup held?
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q1.explanation: The first FIFA World Cup was held in 1930 in Uruguay, who also won the tournament.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q2.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q2.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002), more than any other country.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q3.question: What is 'tiki-taka'?
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option1: A style of play based on short passing and constant movement
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q3.explanation: Tiki-taka is a possession-based playing style emphasizing short passes and fluid movement, made famous by FC Barcelona and the Spanish national team.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q4.question: What does VAR stand for in modern soccer?
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q4.explanation: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee, a system used to review decisions made by the main referee.
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.question: What is a 'false 9' in soccer tactics?
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option1: A goalkeeper who plays as a sweeper
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option2: A center forward who drops deep into midfield to create space
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option3: A defender who joins the attack
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option4: A substitute player who warms up constantly
soccer.quiz.rules-hard.q5.explanation: A false 9 is a center forward who drops into midfield, confusing opposing defenders and creating space for teammates.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.name: Volleyball Basics
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.description: Test your knowledge of basic volleyball rules and facts.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.info1.title: The Game
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.info1.content: Volleyball is played between two teams of 6 players on a court divided by a net. The objective is to send the ball over the net and ground it on the opponent's side. A team is allowed a maximum of 3 touches before sending the ball over the net. Matches are played as best of 5 sets, with sets going to 25 points (or 15 in the deciding set).
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.info2.title: Basic Rules
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.info2.content: The serve starts each rally from behind the back line. Players must rotate positions clockwise each time their team wins the serve back. The ball may not be caught or held — it must be hit cleanly. Touching the net during play is a fault. A point is scored on every rally (rally scoring), regardless of which team served.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q1.question: How many players per team are on the court in volleyball?
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q1.explanatio: A volleyball team has 6 players on the court at a time.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q2.question: How many times may a team touch the ball before sending it over the net?
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q2.explanatio: Each team is allowed a maximum of 3 consecutive touches before the ball must cross the net.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q3.question: How many points do you need to win a regular set?
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q3.explanatio: A regular set is won by the first team to reach 25 points with at least a 2-point lead.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q4.question: What happens when a player touches the net during play?
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q4.option4: It is a fault and the opposing team scores a point
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q4.explanatio: Touching the net while the ball is in play is a fault, giving a point to the opposing team.
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.question: Where must the server stand when serving?
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.option3: At the center of the court
volleyball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.explanatio: The server must stand behind the end line (back line) when striking the ball for the serve.
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.name: Volleyball Rules & Positions
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.description: Dive deeper into volleyball positions, rotations, and rules.
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.title: Positions & Roles
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.conte: The setter is the playmaker who sets up attacks. Outside hitters (left side) are the main attackers. The middle blocker specializes in blocking and quick attacks. The opposite hitter plays right side opposite the setter. The libero is a defensive specialist who wears a different jersey and cannot attack above net height or serve (in some rule sets).
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.title: Rotation & Substitution
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.conte: Players rotate clockwise after winning the serve back (side out). Front-row players (positions 2, 3, 4) can attack from anywhere; back-row players (positions 1, 5, 6) can only attack from behind the 3-meter (attack) line. The libero can replace any back-row player without counting as a substitution. Each team is allowed 6 substitutions per set.
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.question: What is the libero's main role?
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.option3: Defensive specialist who receives and digs
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.explanat: The libero is a back-row defensive specialist focused on receiving serves and digging attacks.
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.question: From where can back-row players attack?
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.option4: Back-row players cannot attack at all
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.explanat: Back-row players may attack but must jump from behind the 3-meter line (also called the attack line).
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.question: When do players rotate positions?
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.option2: When their team wins the serve back (side out)
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.explanat: Players rotate one position clockwise each time their team wins back the right to serve (called a side out).
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.question: How many substitutions per set are allowed?
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.explanat: Each team is allowed a maximum of 6 substitutions per set. Libero replacements do not count.
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.question: Which player typically wears a different colored jersey?
volleyball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.explanat: The libero must wear a contrasting jersey to be easily identified by referees.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.name: Volleyball Advanced Knowledge
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.description: Test your knowledge of volleyball history, advanced rules, and tactics.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.title: History & Origins
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.content: Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan in Holyoke, Massachusetts, USA. He originally called it 'Mintonette.' It was designed as a less physically demanding alternative to basketball. Volleyball became an Olympic sport in 1964 at the Tokyo Games. Beach volleyball was added to the Olympics in 1996.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.title: Advanced Tactics & Techniques
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.content: A 'quick set' (or 'fast') is a low, fast set to the middle hitter to beat the block. The 'pipe' attack is a back-row attack from position 6 (center back). A 'combination play' uses multiple attackers to confuse the opposing blockers. The jump float serve has no spin, making it unpredictable. A 'tool' or 'wipe' is intentionally hitting the ball off the blocker's hands.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.question: In which year was volleyball invented?
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.explanatio: William G. Morgan invented volleyball in 1895, four years after basketball was invented.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.question: What was volleyball originally called?
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.explanatio: Morgan originally called the game 'Mintonette.' It was later renamed 'volleyball' because of the volleying nature of play.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.question: What is a 'pipe' attack?
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option1: A back-row attack from the center back position
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option2: A serve aimed at the middle of the court
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option4: An underhand set to the outside
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.explanatio: A pipe attack is a back-row attack from position 6 (center back), where the player jumps from behind the attack line.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.question: In which year did volleyball become an Olympic sport?
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.explanatio: Indoor volleyball became an Olympic sport at the 1964 Tokyo Games.
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.question: What is a 'tool' or 'wipe' in volleyball?
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option2: Intentionally hitting the ball off the blocker's hands so it goes out
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option3: A defensive technique to save a hard spike
volleyball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.explanatio: A tool (or wipe) is when an attacker intentionally hits the ball off the blocker's hands, causing it to deflect out of bounds.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.name: Handball Basics
handball.quiz.rules-easy.description: Test your knowledge of basic handball rules and facts.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.info1.title: The Game
handball.quiz.rules-easy.info1.content: Handball is played between two teams of 7 players (6 outfield + 1 goalkeeper) on an indoor court. The aim is to throw the ball into the opposing team's goal. A standard match lasts 60 minutes, split into two 30-minute halves. The ball is moved by passing, dribbling, or running with it (maximum 3 steps without dribbling).
handball.quiz.rules-easy.info2.title: Basic Rules
handball.quiz.rules-easy.info2.content: Players may hold the ball for a maximum of 3 seconds before passing, dribbling, or shooting. The 6-meter line marks the goal area — only the goalkeeper is allowed inside. Outfield players may jump into the area to shoot as long as they release the ball before landing. A free throw is taken from where the foul occurred.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q1.question: How many players per team are on the court in handball?
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q1.explanation: A handball team has 7 players on the court: 6 outfield players and 1 goalkeeper.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q2.question: How long is a standard handball match?
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q2.explanation: A standard handball match lasts 60 minutes, divided into two 30-minute halves.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q3.question: How many steps can a player take without dribbling?
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q3.explanation: A player may take a maximum of 3 steps while holding the ball without dribbling.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q4.question: Who is allowed inside the 6-meter goal area?
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q4.explanation: Only the goalkeeper is allowed to stand inside the 6-meter goal area. Other players may jump into it to shoot but must release the ball before landing.
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.question: How long may a player hold the ball without passing, dribbling, or shooting?
handball.quiz.rules-easy.q5.explanation: A player may hold the ball for a maximum of 3 seconds before they must pass, dribble, or shoot.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.name: Handball Rules & Positions
handball.quiz.rules-medium.description: Dive deeper into handball positions, penalties, and rules.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.title: Positions & Roles
handball.quiz.rules-medium.info1.content: The center back is the main playmaker who organizes the attack. Left and right backs are powerful shooters from distance. The pivot (also called line player) operates near the opponent's goal area line, looking for close-range scoring opportunities. The wings play on the sides and specialize in fast breaks and sharp-angle shots.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.title: Penalties & Discipline
handball.quiz.rules-medium.info2.content: A 7-meter throw (penalty) is awarded when a clear scoring opportunity is illegally prevented. Yellow cards serve as warnings. A 2-minute suspension is given for more serious fouls — the team plays with one fewer player. After 3 suspensions, a player receives a red card and is disqualified. The opposing team gets a free throw for most fouls.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.question: What is a 7-meter throw in handball?
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.option3: A penalty throw awarded when a clear scoring chance is illegally prevented
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.option4: The goalkeeper's throw after a save
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q1.explanatio: A 7-meter throw is the handball equivalent of a penalty — it is awarded when a clear goal-scoring opportunity is illegally stopped.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.question: What is the role of the pivot (line player)?
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.option1: To operate near the opponent's goal area line and create close-range scoring chances
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q2.explanatio: The pivot (line player) positions themselves near the 6-meter line to create scoring opportunities from close range.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.question: How long is a suspension in handball?
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q3.explanatio: A suspension lasts 2 minutes, during which the penalized team plays with one fewer player.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.question: After how many 2-minute suspensions does a player get a red card?
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q4.explanatio: After receiving 3 two-minute suspensions, a player is shown a red card and disqualified from the match.
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.question: Which player is known as the main playmaker in handball?
handball.quiz.rules-medium.q5.explanatio: The center back is the primary playmaker who organizes and directs the team's attack.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.name: Handball Advanced Knowledge
handball.quiz.rules-hard.description: Test your knowledge of handball history, advanced rules, and tactics.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.title: History & Origins
handball.quiz.rules-hard.info1.content: Modern handball was developed in Europe in the early 20th century. The game was first codified in 1917 by Max Heiser in Germany. Originally it was played outdoors with 11 players per team (field handball). Indoor handball with 7 players became the standard after World War II. Handball became an Olympic sport for men in 1972 (Munich) and for women in 1976 (Montreal).
handball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.title: Advanced Tactics
handball.quiz.rules-hard.info2.content: Common defensive formations include 6-0 (all six along the 6-meter line), 5-1 (five back, one advanced), and 3-2-1. A 'fast break' (Tempogegenstoss) is a quick counter-attack before the opposing defense is set up. The '7 vs 6' tactic involves replacing the goalkeeper with an extra outfield player. A 'Kempa trick' is a spectacular aerial pass where a player catches and shoots while airborne.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.question: In which year did men's handball become an Olympic sport?
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q1.explanation: Men's indoor handball became an Olympic sport at the 1972 Munich Games.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.question: How many players per team did the original outdoor handball have?
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q2.explanation: The original outdoor version of handball (field handball) was played with 11 players per side, similar to soccer.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.question: What is the 'Kempa trick'?
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.option1: A spectacular move where a player catches a pass and shoots while airborne over the goal area
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q3.explanation: The Kempa trick is named after Bernhard Kempa. The player jumps, receives a pass in mid-air, and shoots before landing in the goal area.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.question: What is the '7 vs 6' tactic?
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.option4: Replacing the goalkeeper with an extra outfield player for a numerical advantage in attack
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q4.explanation: In the 7 vs 6 tactic, the goalkeeper is replaced by an additional outfield player to create a numerical advantage in attack.
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.question: What does a 6-0 defensive formation mean?
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.option2: All 6 defenders positioned along the 6-meter line
handball.quiz.rules-hard.q5.explanation: In a 6-0 defense, all six outfield defenders line up along the 6-meter line, creating a strong defensive wall.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.name: Step Aerobic Basics
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.descriptio: Test your knowledge of basic step aerobic moves and facts.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.info1.cont: Step aerobic is a cardio workout performed on a raised platform (step bench). Participants step up, over, and around the bench in choreographed routines set to music. It improves cardiovascular fitness, coordination, and leg strength. A typical class lasts 30-60 minutes and is suitable for all fitness levels by adjusting the step height.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.info2.cont: The 'basic step' is stepping up with one foot, then the other, then stepping back down. A 'V-step' means stepping wide on the platform to form a V shape. The 'knee lift' involves stepping up and lifting the opposite knee. A 'tap up' means stepping onto the bench and tapping the other foot on top without putting weight on it. Always step with your full foot on the platform.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q1.questio: What piece of equipment is used in step aerobic?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q1.explana: Step aerobic uses a step bench — a raised platform that you step on and off during the workout.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q2.questio: What type of exercise is step aerobic?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q2.explana: Step aerobic is primarily a cardio workout that improves heart and lung fitness while also building coordination.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q3.option3: Stepping wide on the platform to form a V shape
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q3.explana: In a V-step, you step up with feet wide apart on the platform, forming a V shape, then step back to the starting position.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q4.questio: How can you make step aerobic easier or harder?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q4.option4: By adjusting the height of the step bench
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q4.explana: The difficulty is adjusted by raising or lowering the step bench height. A higher step makes the workout more intense.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q5.questio: How should your foot land on the step bench?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q5.option1: With the full foot on the platform
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q5.option4: On the side of the foot
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-easy.q5.explana: You should always place your full foot on the platform to ensure stability and avoid injury.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.name: Step Aerobic Techniques
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.descript: Dive deeper into step aerobic techniques, choreography, and safety.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.info1.co: Step aerobic routines are set to music with a tempo of 118-128 BPM (beats per minute). Moves are organized in counts of 8 (called an '8-count'). A 32-count block is a standard choreography building block. 'Layering' means gradually adding complexity to a basic move. The instructor always leads with verbal and visual cues.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.info2.co: Keep your back straight and core engaged while stepping. Never lock your knees when stepping up. Step close to the bench — don't lean forward too much. Always step down heel-to-toe to absorb impact. Keep your eyes on the step when learning new moves. The recommended step height for beginners is 10-15 cm, for advanced exercisers up to 25 cm.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q1.quest: What is the typical music tempo (BPM) for step aerobic?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q1.expla: Step aerobic music typically runs at 118-128 BPM, which allows for controlled stepping with proper form.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q2.quest: How should you step down from the bench?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q2.optio: Step down heel-to-toe to absorb impact
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q2.expla: Always step down heel-to-toe (toe touches first, then roll to heel) to absorb impact and protect your joints.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q3.optio: Gradually adding complexity to a basic move
Wearing extra clothing during the workout
Repeating the same move over and over
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q3.expla: Layering is a teaching method where the instructor starts with a simple move and gradually adds elements like arm movements, turns, or direction changes.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q4.optio: A standard choreography building block made of 32 music beats
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q4.expla: A 32-count block is the standard unit for building step aerobic choreography, consisting of 32 beats of music (four 8-counts).
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q5.quest: What is the recommended step height for beginners?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-medium.q5.expla: Beginners should use a step height of 10-15 cm to learn proper technique before increasing intensity.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.name: Step Aerobic Advanced Knowledge
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.descriptio: Test your knowledge of step aerobic history, advanced techniques, and training science.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.info1.cont: Step aerobic was created by Gin Miller in 1989 in Atlanta, Georgia, USA. She developed the concept while rehabilitating a knee injury by stepping on and off a milk crate. Reebok partnered with her to commercialize the step platform. Step aerobic became one of the most popular group fitness formats of the 1990s and remains widely practiced today.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.info2.cont: Step aerobic can burn 300-600 calories per hour depending on intensity and step height. The 'over the top' move involves stepping over the bench to the other side. A 'repeater' is doing the same knee lift or kick 3 times before changing feet. 'Propulsion' moves add a jump or hop for higher intensity. Heart rate should stay in the aerobic zone (60-85% of maximum heart rate) for optimal cardiovascular benefit.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q1.explana: Gin Miller created step aerobic in 1989 while rehabilitating a knee injury by stepping up and down on a milk crate.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q2.questio: In which year was step aerobic created?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q2.explana: Gin Miller invented step aerobic in 1989 in Atlanta, Georgia.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q3.option1: Performing the same knee lift or kick 3 times before switching feet
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q3.option2: Repeating the entire routine from the beginning
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q3.option3: Stepping on and off the bench once
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q3.explana: A repeater involves doing the same move (like a knee lift) 3 times on one side before switching, keeping you on the same lead leg.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q4.option3: Movements done lying on the step
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q4.option4: Moves that add a jump or hop for higher intensity
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q4.explana: Propulsion moves incorporate jumps or hops, where both feet leave the ground or the step, significantly increasing workout intensity.
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q5.questio: How many calories per hour can step aerobic burn approximately?
step-aerobic.quiz.basics-hard.q5.explana: Step aerobic can burn approximately 300-600 calories per hour, depending on intensity, step height, and individual body weight.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.name: Basic Basketball Techniques
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.descript: Test your knowledge of dribbling, passing, and shooting fundamentals.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.info1.co: When dribbling, keep the ball below your waist and use your fingertips — not your palm — for better control.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.info2.co: The chest pass and bounce pass are the two most common passes in basketball. Step toward your target for accuracy.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.quest: Which part of the hand should you dribble with?
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.expla: You should dribble with your fingertips for better ball control and feel.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.quest: How high should you bounce the ball when dribbling?
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.expla: Keeping the dribble below your waist makes it harder for defenders to steal the ball.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.quest: What is the most common pass in basketball?
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.expla: The chest pass is the most common and accurate pass in basketball, used for quick, direct deliveries.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.quest: When shooting, where should you aim?
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.expla: Aiming for the back of the rim gives you the best chance of the ball going in, even if your shot is slightly off.
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.quest: What should your follow-through look like when shooting?
basketball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.expla: A proper follow-through has your fingers pointing at the basket, which ensures good arc and backspin.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.name: Intermediate Basketball Skills
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.descri: Challenge yourself with questions about layups, crossovers, and defensive techniques.
A layup from the right side uses a left-foot takeoff and finishes with the right hand. From the left side, it's the opposite.
Good defense starts with a low, wide stance. Keep your arms out and active, stay on the balls of your feet, and slide rather than cross your feet.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.que: In a layup from the right side, which foot do you jump off?
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.exp: When doing a layup from the right side, you jump off your left foot so you can extend your right hand toward the basket.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.opt: Quickly switching the ball from one hand to the other
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.exp: A crossover dribble is a quick change of direction by bouncing the ball from one hand to the other in front of your body.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.que: What is the difference between a chest pass and a bounce pass?
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.opt: A bounce pass bounces on the floor before reaching the teammate
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.exp: The key difference is that a bounce pass bounces off the floor before reaching the receiver, while a chest pass travels through the air directly.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.opt: Low with wide feet and arms out
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.exp: A proper defensive stance means getting low with wide feet and arms extended to cover passing lanes and contest shots.
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.que: What does 'boxing out' mean in rebounding?
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.opt: Using your body to create space between the opponent and the basket
basketball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.exp: Boxing out means positioning your body between an opponent and the basket to gain the best rebounding position after a shot.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.name: Advanced Basketball Techniques
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.descript: Test your knowledge of Euro steps, triple threat, and advanced moves.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.info1.co: The Euro step is a two-step move where the player takes a step in one direction and then quickly steps the other way to evade a defender on the drive to the basket.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.info2.co: The triple threat position is a stance where you hold the ball and can immediately shoot, pass, or dribble. It keeps the defense guessing.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.optio: A two-step move that changes direction to avoid defenders
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.expla: The Euro step is a two-step move where the player shifts direction mid-drive to evade a defender while staying within the rules of traveling.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.optio: A jump shot while leaning backward
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.expla: A fadeaway is a jump shot where the player leans backward while shooting, creating space from the defender.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.quest: What is the triple threat position?
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.optio: A stance from which you can shoot, pass, or dribble
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.expla: The triple threat position allows you to immediately shoot, pass, or dribble, keeping your defender unsure of your next move.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.optio: Passing while looking in a different direction
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.expla: A no-look pass is delivered while the passer looks in another direction to deceive the defense about the intended target.
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.quest: What is the purpose of a pump fake?
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.optio: To trick the defender into jumping by faking a shot
basketball.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.expla: A pump fake mimics a shooting motion to get the defender to jump, creating an opening to drive or shoot uncontested.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.name: Famous Basketball Players — Basics
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.description: How well do you know the biggest names in basketball history?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.info1.title: Michael Jordan
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.info1.content: Michael Jordan, wearing #23, won 6 NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls. He is widely considered the greatest basketball player of all time.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.info2.title: NBA & WNBA Basics
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.info2.content: The NBA (National Basketball Association) is the top men's professional league. The WNBA (Women's National Basketball Association) is the top women's league in the USA.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q1.question: Which team did Michael Jordan famously play for?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q1.explanatio: Michael Jordan spent most of his legendary career with the Chicago Bulls, winning all six of his championships there.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q2.question: What jersey number is most associated with Michael Jordan?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q2.explanatio: The number 23 is iconic and synonymous with Michael Jordan. It has been retired by the Chicago Bulls in his honor.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q3.question: What does NBA stand for?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q3.explanatio: NBA stands for the National Basketball Association, the premier professional basketball league in North America.
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q4.question: How many NBA championships did Michael Jordan win?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q4.explanatio: Michael Jordan won 6 NBA championships, all with the Chicago Bulls, in two three-peats (1991-93 and 1996-98).
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q5.question: What is the women's professional basketball league in the USA called?
basketball.quiz.stars-easy.q5.explanatio: The WNBA (Women's National Basketball Association) is the premier professional women's basketball league in the United States.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.name: Famous Basketball Players — Deeper Knowledge
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.description: Dive deeper into the lives and careers of basketball legends.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.info1.title: LeBron James
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.info1.conte: LeBron James, known as 'King James,' has won 4 NBA MVP awards and multiple championships with different teams. He became the NBA's all-time leading scorer.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.info2.title: Kobe Bryant
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.info2.conte: Kobe Bryant spent his entire 20-year career with the LA Lakers, winning 5 championships. He wore #8 early on and #24 for the latter half of his career.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q1.question: LeBron James is known by what nickname?
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q1.explanat: LeBron James earned the nickname 'King James' for his dominant, all-around play since entering the league.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q2.question: What legendary number did Kobe Bryant wear for most of his career with the Lakers?
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q2.explanat: Kobe Bryant wore #24 for the majority of his career. Both #8 and #24 were retired by the Lakers.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q3.question: Which player was famous for the 'Magic' in their name and played for the LA Lakers?
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q3.explanat: Earvin 'Magic' Johnson was one of the greatest point guards in NBA history, leading the Lakers' Showtime era in the 1980s.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q4.question: Larry Bird played for which team his entire career?
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q4.explanat: Larry Bird spent his entire 13-year career with the Boston Celtics, winning 3 NBA championships and 3 consecutive MVP awards.
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q5.question: How many NBA MVP awards did LeBron James win?
basketball.quiz.stars-medium.q5.explanat: LeBron James has won 4 NBA MVP awards (2009, 2010, 2012, 2013), showcasing his sustained dominance.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.name: Basketball Records & History
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.description: Only true basketball experts will ace this quiz on records and legends.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.info1.title: All-Time Records
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.info1.content: The NBA has a rich history of incredible records — from Wilt Chamberlain's 100-point game to Bill Russell's 11 championship rings.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.info2.title: Basketball Legends
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.info2.content: Players like Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, LeBron James, and Wilt Chamberlain have left a lasting impact on the game through their extraordinary achievements.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q1.question: Who holds the record for most points in a single NBA game with 100?
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q1.explanatio: Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points in a single game on March 2, 1962 — a record that still stands today.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q2.question: Who won the most NBA championship rings as a player with 11?
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q2.explanatio: Bill Russell won an incredible 11 NBA championships with the Boston Celtics between 1957 and 1969.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q3.question: What signature move is Kareem Abdul-Jabbar famous for?
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q3.explanatio: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's skyhook was virtually unblockable and helped him become one of the greatest scorers in NBA history.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q4.question: Who is the NBA's all-time leading scorer?
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q4.explanatio: LeBron James surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to become the NBA's all-time leading scorer in 2023.
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q5.question: Which player became the youngest NBA MVP in history?
basketball.quiz.stars-hard.q5.explanatio: Derrick Rose won the NBA MVP award in 2011 at age 22, making him the youngest player to receive the honor.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.name: Basic Soccer Techniques
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.description: Test your knowledge of passing, ball control, and basic skills.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.info1.title: Passing Basics
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.info1.conten: The inside of the foot is the most reliable surface for short, accurate passes. Keep your ankle firm and follow through toward your target.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.info2.title: Ball Control
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.info2.conten: Good ball control — also called a 'first touch' — means receiving the ball and keeping it close to your body so you can make your next move quickly.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.question: Which part of the foot is best for accurate short passes?
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.explanati: The inside of the foot provides the largest flat surface for contact, giving you the most accuracy on short passes.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.question: What does 'trapping' the ball mean?
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.explanati: Trapping means stopping and controlling the ball, usually with your foot, thigh, or chest, so you can play it next.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.question: When heading the ball, which part of the head should you use?
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.explanati: The forehead is the hardest and flattest part of the head, giving you the best control and power when heading.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.question: What is the basic dribbling technique?
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.option4: Use small touches to keep the ball close
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.explanati: Dribbling uses small, controlled touches to keep the ball close to your feet so you can change direction quickly.
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.question: What does 'first touch' mean in soccer?
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.option2: The first contact you make to control a pass
soccer.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.explanati: Your first touch is the initial contact you make to control an incoming ball — a good first touch sets up your next play.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.name: Intermediate Soccer Skills
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.descriptio: Test your knowledge of shooting, defending, and tactical techniques.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.info1.cont: For a powerful shot, strike the ball with your instep (laces). Plant your non-kicking foot beside the ball, lean slightly over it, and follow through.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.info2.cont: Good defending means staying between the ball and your goal, watching the ball rather than the attacker's body, and timing your tackles carefully.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.questio: For a powerful shot, which part of the foot do you use?
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.explana: The instep (laces) provides the most power and a larger striking surface for shots on goal.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.option1: A quick pass to a teammate who passes it back
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.option2: Kicking the ball against a wall
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.explana: A wall pass (one-two) is a quick combination where you pass to a teammate and run past the defender to receive the return pass.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.option2: Using your body to protect the ball from an opponent
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.explana: Shielding means positioning your body between the ball and the opponent to protect possession.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.option4: Sliding on the ground to take the ball from an opponent
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.explana: A sliding tackle is a defensive move where a player slides along the ground to knock the ball away from an attacker.
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.option1: A pass from a wide position into the penalty area
soccer.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.explana: A cross is a long pass from the wing into the penalty area, typically aimed at a teammate for a header or volley.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.name: Advanced Soccer Techniques
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.description: Do you know the trickiest moves in soccer? Test your expertise here.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.info1.title: Famous Trick Moves
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.info1.conten: Soccer has many iconic moves — the Cruyff turn, the rabona, and the nutmeg are all crowd favorites that require great skill and timing.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.info2.title: Advanced Shooting
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.info2.conten: Advanced shots like the bicycle kick and trivela require exceptional technique. The bicycle kick involves flipping backward to strike the ball overhead.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.question: What is a 'rabona'?
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.option2: Kicking the ball by wrapping your leg behind your standing leg
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.explanati: A rabona is a trick where you kick the ball by crossing your kicking leg behind your standing leg — a flashy and difficult skill.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.question: What is a bicycle kick (overhead kick)?
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.option3: An acrobatic kick where you flip backward and kick the ball overhead
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.explanati: A bicycle kick is a spectacular acrobatic move where the player flips backward in the air to kick the ball overhead.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.question: The 'Cruyff turn' is named after which Dutch player?
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.explanati: The Cruyff turn was popularized by the legendary Dutch player Johan Cruyff during the 1974 World Cup.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.question: What is a 'trivela'?
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.option4: A shot or pass using the outside of the foot with curve
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.explanati: A trivela is a technique where the ball is struck with the outside of the foot, producing a curving trajectory — difficult but very effective.
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.question: What does 'nutmeg' mean in soccer?
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.option2: Passing the ball through an opponent's legs
soccer.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.explanati: A nutmeg is when you play the ball through an opponent's legs and collect it on the other side — one of the most humiliating moves in soccer.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.name: Famous Soccer Players — Basics
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.description: How well do you know the biggest stars in world soccer?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.info1.title: Lionel Messi
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.info1.content: Lionel Messi from Argentina is considered one of the greatest soccer players ever. He won the World Cup in 2022 and holds the record for the most Ballon d'Or awards.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.info2.title: Cristiano Ronaldo
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.info2.content: Cristiano Ronaldo from Portugal is one of the top scorers in soccer history. He has won multiple Champions League titles and league championships.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q1.question: Which country is Lionel Messi from?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q1.explanation: Lionel Messi was born in Rosario, Argentina, and has represented Argentina throughout his international career.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q2.question: Which country is Cristiano Ronaldo from?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q2.explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo was born on the island of Madeira, Portugal, and is the captain of the Portuguese national team.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q3.question: How often is the FIFA World Cup held?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q3.explanation: The FIFA World Cup is held every 4 years, making it one of the most anticipated sporting events in the world.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q4.question: Which tournament is the biggest in club soccer in Europe?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q4.explanation: The UEFA Champions League is the most prestigious club competition in European soccer, featuring the top teams from across the continent.
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q5.question: What color card means a player is sent off?
soccer.quiz.stars-easy.q5.explanation: A red card means the player is immediately sent off the field and cannot be replaced — their team plays with one fewer player.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.name: Famous Soccer Players — Deeper Knowledge
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.description: Dive deeper into the careers of soccer's greatest legends.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.info1.title: Pelé
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.info1.content: Pelé, the Brazilian legend, won 3 FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970) and scored over 1,000 career goals. He is often called the greatest player of all time.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.info2.title: Diego Maradona
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.info2.content: Diego Maradona of Argentina led his country to the 1986 World Cup title. His 'Hand of God' goal and solo 'Goal of the Century' in the same match are legendary.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q1.question: How many World Cups did Pelé win with Brazil?
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q1.explanation: Pelé won 3 World Cups with Brazil (1958, 1962, and 1970), a record that no other player has matched.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q2.question: Which famous goal by Maradona is known as the 'Hand of God'?
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q2.option1: A goal scored using his hand in the 1986 World Cup
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q2.explanation: In the 1986 World Cup quarter-final against England, Maradona punched the ball into the net with his hand — he later called it 'the Hand of God.'
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q3.question: Zinedine Zidane is from which country?
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q3.explanation: Zinedine Zidane is from France and led Les Bleus to World Cup victory in 1998 and the European Championship in 2000.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q4.question: David Beckham was famous for which skill?
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q4.explanation: David Beckham was renowned for his ability to bend free kicks and deliver pinpoint crosses from the right wing.
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q5.question: Which country has the most World Cup titles?
soccer.quiz.stars-medium.q5.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002).
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.name: Soccer Records & Legends
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.description: Only true soccer experts will ace this quiz on records and all-time greats.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.info1.title: World Cup Records
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.info1.content: The FIFA World Cup has been held since 1930. Miroslav Klose holds the record for most World Cup goals (16), and Pelé is the youngest player to score in a final.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.info2.title: The Ballon d'Or
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.info2.content: The Ballon d'Or is the most prestigious individual award in soccer. Lionel Messi holds the record for the most wins. It has been awarded annually since 1956.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q1.question: Who holds the record for most goals in World Cup history?
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q1.explanation: Germany's Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups (2002-2014), making him the all-time leading scorer in World Cup history.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q2.question: Which player has won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q2.explanation: Lionel Messi has won the most Ballon d'Or awards, cementing his status as one of the greatest players in soccer history.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q3.question: In which year was the first FIFA World Cup held?
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q3.explanation: The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930, with 13 teams participating. Uruguay won the inaugural tournament.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q4.question: Who is the youngest player to score in a World Cup final?
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q4.explanation: Pelé was just 17 years old when he scored in the 1958 World Cup final, making him the youngest player to do so.
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q5.question: Which country won the first ever World Cup?
soccer.quiz.stars-hard.q5.explanation: Uruguay won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final held in Montevideo.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.name: Basic Volleyball Techniques
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.descript: Test your knowledge of fundamental volleyball skills like serving, passing, and setting.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.info1.co: The underhand serve is the most beginner-friendly serve in volleyball. Stand with your non-dominant foot slightly forward, hold the ball in your non-dominant hand at waist height, and swing your dominant hand in a fist to strike the ball from below. Keep your eyes on the ball and follow through toward the target.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.info2.co: The bump pass, also called a forearm pass, is the most basic way to receive a serve or dig a ball. Clasp your hands together with arms straight, creating a flat platform with your forearms. Contact the ball on your forearms above the wrists, and use your legs to direct the ball by angling your platform toward the target.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.quest: In an underhand serve, which hand holds the ball?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.optio: Neither hand — the ball is tossed first
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.expla: When performing an underhand serve, you hold the ball steady in your non-dominant hand and strike it with your dominant hand.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.quest: What is another name for the forearm pass?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.expla: The forearm pass is commonly called a 'bump' because the ball bounces off the forearms.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.quest: In a set, where should the ball contact your hands?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.expla: A proper set uses the fingertips of both hands to push the ball upward with precision and control.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.quest: What is the ready position in volleyball?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.optio: Standing straight with arms at your sides
Knees bent, arms in front of your body
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.expla: The ready position means knees are bent, weight is forward on the balls of your feet, and arms are out in front, ready to react quickly.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.quest: When bumping, where should the ball contact your arms?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.expla: The ball should contact the flat platform of your forearms just above the wrists for a controlled and accurate pass.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.name: Intermediate Volleyball Skills
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.descri: Test your knowledge of spiking, blocking, and other intermediate volleyball techniques.
The standard spike approach uses three steps: left-right-left for right-handed players (or right-left-right for left-handed). The first step is a timing step, the second is a long power step, and the third is a quick closing step to convert forward momentum into vertical jump height. Your arms swing back and then forward to help generate maximum jump power.
Blocking is the first line of defense at the net. Stand facing the net with hands at shoulder height. Shuffle sideways to position yourself in front of the attacker, then jump with both feet and press your hands over and across the net into the opponent's side. Keep your fingers spread and rigid to create a large blocking surface.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.que: How many steps is the standard spike approach?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.exp: The standard spiking approach consists of 3 steps: a timing step, a power step, and a closing step before the jump.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.que: When blocking, should your hands go over the net?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.opt: Yes, push your hands over and toward the opponent's court
No, keep them on your side of the net
Only one hand should go over
Hands should stay below the top of the net
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.exp: Effective blocking requires pressing your hands over the net and into the opponent's side to deflect the ball downward.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.opt: A very fast and powerful serve
A serve with no spin that moves unpredictably in the air
A serve that bounces before crossing the net
An underhand serve with a gentle arc
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.exp: A float serve is struck with no spin, causing it to wobble and drift unpredictably, making it difficult for receivers to judge.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.que: What is the proper footwork for a block?
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.opt: Backpedal away from the net, then jump
Shuffle sideways to position, then jump with both feet
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.exp: Blockers use a lateral shuffle to get in position and then jump off both feet for maximum height and balance.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.opt: A defensive play to keep a hard-driven ball from hitting the floor
An attack from the back row
A set to the middle hitter
volleyball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.exp: A dig is a defensive skill used to receive a hard-driven attack and keep the ball in play, often requiring quick reflexes and low body position.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.name: Advanced Volleyball Techniques
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.descript: Challenge yourself with questions about jump serves, back-row attacks, and advanced offensive plays.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.info1.co: The jump serve is the most aggressive serve in volleyball. The player tosses the ball high into the air, takes an approach similar to a spike, jumps, and strikes the ball at the peak of the jump with a full arm swing. This generates tremendous power and topspin, making it very difficult to receive. Top players can serve at over 120 km/h.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.info2.co: Advanced offenses use combination plays to confuse blockers. The setter dump is a surprise second-contact attack by the setter. The roll shot is a softer, controlled off-speed attack using topspin to clear blockers. The pancake is an emergency defensive technique where a player slides their hand flat on the floor to let the ball bounce up off the back of the hand.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.optio: Tossing the ball high, then jumping to hit it with power like a spike
A float serve with a small hop
An underhand serve with a jump
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.expla: A jump serve involves tossing the ball into the air, approaching and jumping like a spike, and hitting with maximum power and topspin.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.optio: An attack from the front row near the net
An attack launched from behind the 3-meter line
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.expla: A back-row attack is when a player in the back row jumps from behind the 3-meter (attack) line to hit the ball, adding an extra offensive threat.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.optio: When the setter tips or pushes the ball over on the second contact instead of setting
A bad set that goes into the net
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.expla: A setter dump is a deceptive play where the setter attacks the ball on the second contact instead of setting a hitter, catching the defense off guard.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.optio: A failed pass that goes out of bounds
A cooking technique for team meals
Laying your hand flat on the floor so the ball bounces off the back of it
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q4.expla: A pancake dig is a last-resort defensive move where the player slides their hand flat on the floor, allowing the ball to bounce up off the back of the hand.
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.optio: Rolling on the floor to save the ball
A softer off-speed attack that uses topspin to clear the blockers
A type of serve with sidespin
volleyball.quiz.techniques-hard.q5.expla: A roll shot is a controlled, off-speed attack where the hitter uses wrist action to put topspin on the ball, sending it over or around the block with less power but more precision.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.name: Famous Volleyball Facts
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.description: Test your general knowledge about volleyball at the Olympics and around the world.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.info1.title: Volleyball at the Olympics
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.info1.content: Indoor volleyball has been an Olympic sport since 1964 in Tokyo. Beach volleyball was added to the Olympic program in 1996 at the Atlanta Games. Both men's and women's tournaments are held in each edition of the Summer Olympics, making volleyball one of the most popular Olympic team sports worldwide.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.info2.title: Beach vs. Indoor Volleyball
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.info2.content: While indoor volleyball features 6 players per side on a hard court, beach volleyball is played 2-on-2 on sand. Beach volleyball uses a slightly larger and lighter ball, and matches are best of 3 sets instead of 5. There is no libero in beach volleyball, and players must be skilled in all areas of the game since there are only two of them.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q1.question: How many players are on each side in beach volleyball?
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q1.explanatio: Beach volleyball is played with teams of just 2 players on each side.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q2.question: In which country is volleyball extremely popular as a national sport?
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q2.explanatio: Brazil is one of the most passionate volleyball nations in the world, excelling in both indoor and beach volleyball.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q3.question: Is volleyball played in the Summer or Winter Olympics?
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q3.explanatio: Volleyball is a Summer Olympics sport, with both indoor and beach volleyball featured.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q4.question: Which surface is beach volleyball played on?
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q4.explanatio: Beach volleyball is played on sand, which makes movement more challenging and is part of what makes the sport unique.
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q5.question: How many sets does a team need to win to win an indoor volleyball match?
volleyball.quiz.stars-easy.q5.explanatio: Indoor volleyball matches are best of 5 sets, so a team needs to win 3 sets to win the match.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.name: Volleyball Stars & Nations
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.description: Test your knowledge of famous volleyball players and successful national teams.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.info1.title: Karch Kiraly — The Legend
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.info1.conte: Karch Kiraly is widely regarded as the greatest volleyball player of all time. He won Olympic gold in indoor volleyball in 1984 and 1988 with the USA, then added a gold medal in beach volleyball in 1996 — making him the only person to win Olympic gold in both disciplines. He later became head coach of the US women's national team.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.info2.title: Famous Volleyball Nations
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.info2.conte: Brazil has been a dominant force in both men's and women's volleyball, producing legendary players like Giba and Jaqueline. The USA, Russia (formerly Soviet Union), and Japan are also traditional powerhouses. China's women's team, coached by Olympic champion Lang Ping, has won multiple world titles.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q1.question: Karch Kiraly is the only person to win Olympic gold in both indoor volleyball and what?
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q1.explanat: Karch Kiraly uniquely won Olympic gold in both indoor volleyball (1984, 1988) and beach volleyball (1996).
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q2.question: Which country has won the most men's Olympic volleyball gold medals?
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q2.explanat: Brazil has been the most successful men's volleyball nation at the Olympics, with multiple gold medals.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q3.question: Lang Ping is a famous volleyball coach and player from which country?
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q3.explanat: Lang Ping, nicknamed the 'Iron Hammer,' is a Chinese volleyball legend who won Olympic gold as a player and later as a coach.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q4.question: Giba, one of volleyball's greatest players, is from which country?
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q4.explanat: Gilberto Godoy Filho, known as Giba, is a Brazilian volleyball legend who helped Brazil win the 2004 Olympic gold medal.
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q5.question: Which country hosted the first official volleyball competition at the Olympics in 1964?
volleyball.quiz.stars-medium.q5.explanat: The 1964 Tokyo Olympics in Japan were the first to include volleyball as an official Olympic sport.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.name: Volleyball Records & History
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.description: Test your deep knowledge of volleyball records, rule changes, and historical milestones.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.info1.title: Volleyball Records
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.info1.content: The rally point scoring system, introduced in 1999, changed volleyball dramatically — every rally now results in a point, regardless of which team served. This made matches faster and more exciting for spectators. Before this change, only the serving team could score points (side-out scoring).
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.info2.title: Rule Evolution
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.info2.content: The libero position was introduced in 1998 to improve defensive play and make rallies longer. The original game was called 'Mintonette' when William G. Morgan invented it in 1895. The USA men's team has been one of the most decorated Olympic volleyball teams in history, winning 3 gold medals.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q1.question: In which year was the rally point scoring system introduced in volleyball?
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q1.explanatio: The rally point scoring system was introduced in 1999, replacing the old side-out scoring where only the serving team could score.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q2.question: How many Olympic gold medals has the USA men's volleyball team won?
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q2.explanatio: The USA men's volleyball team has won 3 Olympic gold medals, making them one of the most successful teams in Olympic volleyball history.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q3.question: The libero position was introduced to international volleyball rules in which year?
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q3.explanatio: The libero was introduced in 1998 to strengthen the back-row defense and make rallies longer and more exciting.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q4.question: What was the original name for volleyball when it was invented?
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q4.explanatio: William G. Morgan originally called his new game 'Mintonette' in 1895. It was later renamed 'volleyball' because players volleyed the ball back and forth.
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q5.question: Which country has won the most women's Olympic volleyball gold medals?
volleyball.quiz.stars-hard.q5.explanatio: China has been one of the most successful women's volleyball nations at the Olympics, with multiple gold medals.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.name: Basic Handball Techniques
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.descriptio: Test your knowledge of fundamental handball skills like throwing, catching, and dribbling.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.info1.cont: The overhand throw is the most basic and commonly used throw in handball. Grip the ball with one hand, bring your arm back behind your head, step forward with the opposite foot, and throw the ball forward with a snapping wrist motion. Your elbow should lead the throw, and you should follow through toward the target for accuracy.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.info2.cont: Proper catching is essential in handball. Form a 'W' shape with both hands by spreading your fingers and pointing your thumbs toward each other. Reach toward the ball, cushion it on contact by giving slightly with your hands, and secure it immediately. Always watch the ball into your hands and be ready to transition quickly to a throw or dribble.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.questio: What is the most basic throw in handball?
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q1.explana: The overhand throw is the most fundamental and widely used throwing technique in handball.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.questio: How should you catch a ball in handball?
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.option3: With both hands forming a W-shape
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q2.explana: The correct catching technique uses both hands forming a W-shape with spread fingers and thumbs pointing toward each other.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q3.explana: Yes, dribbling is allowed in handball and works similarly to basketball. Players bounce the ball while moving to advance past defenders.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.questio: What happens if you hold the ball too long in handball?
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.option3: A goal is awarded to the other team
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.option4: It's a violation — the other team gets the ball
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q4.explana: Holding the ball for more than 3 seconds without passing, dribbling, or shooting is a violation, and possession is given to the opposing team.
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.questio: How do you protect the ball when a defender attacks?
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.option2: Shield it with your body and turn away from the defender
handball.quiz.techniques-easy.q5.explana: When under pressure, you should shield the ball with your body by turning away from the defender, keeping the ball protected while looking for a passing option.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.name: Intermediate Handball Skills
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.descript: Test your knowledge of jump shots, defensive formations, and other intermediate handball techniques.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.info1.co: The jump shot is one of handball's most spectacular techniques. The player takes a running approach, jumps off one foot (opposite to the throwing arm), and releases the ball at the highest point of the jump. This gives the shooter an elevated angle over the defense and more time to pick a corner of the goal. The jump shot is especially effective from the back court positions.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.info2.co: Handball defenses use various formations to stop the opposing attack. The 6-0 places all six defenders along the 6-meter line. The 5-1 has five back and one advanced defender pressing the playmaker. The 3-2-1 is an aggressive formation with defenders at three levels. The choice of defense depends on the opponent's strengths and the team's own defensive capabilities.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.quest: When performing a jump shot, when do you release the ball?
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.optio: At the highest point of your jump
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q1.expla: The ball should be released at the highest point of the jump to get the best angle over the defense and maximum power.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.optio: A shot where you spin your body to generate power and change the angle
Spinning the ball in your hand before throwing
A goalkeeper save where the keeper spins
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q2.expla: A spin shot involves rotating your body during the throwing motion to generate extra power and change the release angle, making it harder for the goalkeeper to anticipate.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.quest: What is a 'fast break' in handball?
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.optio: A quick counter-attack before the defense can set up
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q3.expla: A fast break is a rapid counter-attack launched immediately after gaining possession, aiming to score before the opposing defense can organize.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.quest: What is the hip shot used for?
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.optio: A quick low shot from hip height, useful from the wing
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q4.expla: The hip shot is released from hip height without a full arm swing, making it a fast and deceptive shot especially useful for wing players shooting from tight angles.
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.quest: What does the center back player typically do?
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.optio: Directs the offense and creates scoring opportunities
Only blocks shots at the goal
Stays on the bench as a substitute
handball.quiz.techniques-medium.q5.expla: The center back is the team's main playmaker, organizing the attack, distributing the ball, and creating scoring chances for teammates.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.name: Advanced Handball Techniques
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.descriptio: Challenge yourself with questions about the Kempa trick, advanced goalkeeper play, and expert-level tactics.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.info1.cont: The Kempa trick, named after German player Bernhard Kempa, is one of the most spectacular moves in handball. A teammate throws the ball to a player who is already airborne over the goal area. The airborne player catches the ball and shoots before landing — all while in mid-air. This move is legal because the player was airborne before entering the goal area.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.info2.cont: Elite goalkeepers rely on reading the shooter's body language, arm angle, and approach to anticipate where the shot will go. They use a combination of reflexive saves, positional play, and psychological intimidation. Modern goalkeepers also play a key role in initiating fast breaks with quick and accurate outlet passes.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.option1: A penalty fake to fool the goalkeeper
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.option2: Catching a pass while airborne and shooting before landing
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.option3: A dribble move to get past a defender
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q1.explana: The Kempa trick involves a player jumping into the air, catching a pass from a teammate, and shooting the ball before landing — a spectacular mid-air play.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.option1: A hard, powerful throw at the goal
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.option2: A block at the goal line
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.option3: A soft arcing shot that goes over the goalkeeper
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q2.explana: An underhand lob is a soft, arcing shot released from below that floats over the goalkeeper's reach, used when the keeper is positioned low or comes out aggressively.
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.questio: What makes a goalkeeper's reflexive save effective?
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.option1: Reading the shooter's body language and arm angle
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.option2: Closing their eyes to focus on instinct
handball.quiz.techniques-hard.q3.option4: Only using their hands, never their legs
